rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use visible identifiers from the current target component or its visible context
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they relate to component type features
Place assignments in implementation annexes when they relate to implementation-specific subcomponents or connections
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the most specific valid scope that contains all referenced identifiers
Ensure all identifiers in an AGREE annex are visible in the annex's scope
Avoid mixing component type and implementation-specific identifiers in the same annex block
Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume` and `guarantee` for component type contracts
Wrap AGREE contracts in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees in implementation annexes unless they are implementation-specific
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not mix component type and implementation-specific identifiers in the same annex block
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
